answers.b: With weight management and physical activity

explanations.a: Incorrect. Diet changes alone are part of treatment, but not the complete approach.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Weight management and exercise are part of treatment, but not the complete approach.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Medication (statins) is part of treatment, but not the complete approach.
explanations.d: Correct. High cholesterol is typically treated with a combination of diet changes, weight management, physical activity, and medication (if needed).

questions.question: 2. Increase in levels of cholesterol results-

explanations.a: Incorrect. Hypocholesterolemia is low cholesterol, not high.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Cardiovascular disease is a result, but more specific is atherosclerosis.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Cerebral hemorrhage is not directly caused by high cholesterol.
explanations.d: Correct. Increased cholesterol levels result in atherosclerosis - the buildup of plaque in artery walls, which can lead to cardiovascular disease.

questions.question: 3. What is the recommended LDL cholesterol level for most adults?

explanations.a: Correct. The optimal LDL level for most adults is less than 100 mg/dL. Levels above 160 mg/dL are considered high.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Less than 130 mg/dL is near optimal/above optimal range.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Less than 160 mg/dL is borderline high.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Less than 200 mg/dL is the desirable total cholesterol level, not LDL.

questions.question: 4. Which type of fat is most likely to raise LDL cholesterol levels?

explanations.a: Incorrect. Monounsaturated fats (found in olive oil, avocado) can actually help lower LDL cholesterol.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Polyunsaturated fats (found in fish, nuts) can help lower LDL cholesterol.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Saturated fats raise LDL cholesterol, but trans fats are worse.
explanations.d: Correct. Trans fats (partially hydrogenated oils) are the most harmful and raise LDL cholesterol while also lowering HDL cholesterol.

questions.question: 5. Which of the following lifestyle changes can help increase HDL ('good') cholesterol?

answers.c: Eating foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids

explanations.a: Incorrect. Regular exercise is one way to increase HDL, but not the only one.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Quitting smoking improves HDL levels, but not the only way.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Omega-3 fatty acids help increase HDL, but not the only way.
explanations.d: Correct. All of the above - regular physical exercise, quitting smoking, and eating foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids - can help increase HDL cholesterol levels.
